name: Crossbar
region: A mill-side depot on the road south of Thornfield

sensoryProfile.sight: A barn wall with the name painted fresh, balanced books, ordinary grain sacks, and a storehouse too clean of error to feel honest.
sensoryProfile.sound: Thin evening quiet with almost no labor noise, as if routine had been laid over the place after the real work moved on.
sensoryProfile.smell: Ordinary grain, old burlap, and mouse droppings in the corners.
sensoryProfile.touch: Ledger pages without the usual wear of correction and figures left too perfectly matched.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: False normalcy. The surface is intact, but the life has been edited out of it.

establishedFacts.fact: Crossbar's books are so balanced and margin-free that Sylvanas reads them as arranged rather than trustworthy.
The load through this stop is nearly half the prior year's volume for the same month.
The stop suggests the enemy can pull tainted loads back off a hot road and let clean grain continue through as cover.

eventsHere.event: The party concludes they are reading a road after the wagons have already passed and the scrub has begun.
